sub_mechanisms: M_1: per-layer non-commutative moment transform from gradient statistics
M_2: compute non-commutative cumulants (additive form) from the moments
M_3: subtract cross-layer cumulant contributions to decorrelate
M_4: apply decorrelated residual gradient as update
stripped_llm_application: Optimizer computes a non-commutative S-transform per layer from gradient statistics, derives additive non-commutative cumulants, subtracts cross-layer cumulant contributions, and applies the decorrelated residual as the layer's gradient update.
novelty_claim_stripped: Classical decorrelation methods (CDM, batch-norm, NTK regularization) use classical cumulants and Gaussian-approximation. Free-probability cumulants for cross-layer NN gradient decorrelation are new.
